Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping (Xi Jinping) is the current Prime Minister of China and the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China. His biography can be analyzed in the following steps:

1. Birth and Childhood (1953-1968)
Birth: August 18, 1953, effectively in the city of Beijing. His father was a Communist Party official and his mother was a teacher.
Childhood and Education: He enrolled at Peking University in chemistry (in 1969). During his education, China’s Cultural Revolution (1966–1976) took place, where he worked in agriculture and worked in various places during the Cultural Revolution.
2. Career and Political Rise (1969-2007)
1969-1975: He worked in agriculture in Shaanxi province and later worked in several factories in Shaanxi province.
1975-1982: He worked in the propaganda department of the Communist Party of China and later worked in Shanxi province.
1982-1985: He became a local official in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
1985-1992: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
1992-1998: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
1998-2002: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
2002-2007: He became vice-chairman of the local government in Shanxi province and later worked in the local government of Shanxi province.
3. Political Rise (2007-2013)
2007: He became the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China and later China Communist
